ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the algorithm run */,
  "popsize" INTEGER /* Population size used in the genetic algorithm */,
  "generation" INTEGER /* Generation number of the algorithm run */,
  "best" TEXT /* Best performance value achieved in the generation */,
  "average" TEXT /* Average performance value across the generation */,
  "stand" TEXT /* Standard deviation of performance values in the generation */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Total execution time for the generation */,
  "convergence" REAL /* Convergence metric for the generation */,
  "fitness_function_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fitness function used */,
  "additional_metrics" TEXT /* Additional performance metrics (e.g., worst value, median value) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm run */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the run */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used */,
  "popsize" INTEGER /* Population size used in the run */,
  "generation" INTEGER /* Generation number for the run */,
  "run_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the algorithm was executed */,
  "config_details" TEXT /* Configuration details for the algorithm run */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the run (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Details about the environment where the run was executed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, researcher, developer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithms" (
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the algorithm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the algorithm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fitness_functions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fitness function */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the fitness function */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the fitness function */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
